content:
Kate started some new pills. Mood stabilizers. And it's scary. 
She was talking about how feelings hit but the emotion just flickers and disappears. She struggles to name the emotions. Logically she knows she's feeling them because she knows how her body is reacting but mentally it's just not there. The lack of emotion is causing her not to care about anything. Shes not apathetic but she's worried she'll become that way. She's not sure she can align her actions with her values. Her mannerisms have changed. So has the way she communicates. She seems like a completely different person. 
And the scariest part is that person is me. 
I don't like it. Don't get me wrong there are positives for sure. The house has never been cleaner. We don't fight. Talking is so much easier. Everyone is ready early. 
But it's not her. It's like her soul has left and a new being is inhabiting her body. 
It's only a week in so maybe it'll settle into a happy middle ground. But I don't know... I'd rather have all the problems and have my wife back... This isn't the person I fell in love with. 
I don't want her to suffer all the same identity and masking issues I have. It's not worth it. If it was as easy as stopping taking a pill I would have done it years ago..
